title: RX_TIMEOUT_MS of 5000ms may starve other work
description:
lora_l2.c:42: #define RX_TIMEOUT_MS 5000

The RX thread blocks in lora_recv for up to 5 seconds per iteration. During this time, the running flag is not checked (it's only checked at loop top).

What goes wrong: lichen_lora_l2_stop() sets running=0 and joins the thread. If the thread is blocked in lora_recv, stop waits up to 5.1 seconds (100ms + 5000ms join timeouts).

This is documented and handled (abort after timeout), but 5 seconds is long for a stop operation. A user calling disable/enable in quick succession may see unexpected delays.

Fix: Consider shorter RX timeout (500-1000ms) with the understanding that more loop iterations means more running-flag checks. Or expose via Kconfig. The current 5s is reasonable for battery savings but poor for responsiveness.
close_reason: Fixed: Reduced RX_TIMEOUT_MS from 5000ms to 1000ms (default) via new CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2_RX_TIMEOUT_MS Kconfig option. Stop latency reduced from 5.1s to 1.1s worst case. Users can adjust via Kconfig (100-10000ms range).
